mnemonics: M.A.S.S. Storage: Magnetic media, Addressing, Scheduling, and Structure
keyPoints: Disk Scheduling Algorithms (FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK, C-LOOK) optimize head movement
The operating system is responsible for bad block management and disk formatting
RAID (Redundant Array of Independent Disks) levels provide various combinations of performance, capacity, and fault tolerance
commonMistakes: Assuming SSTF (Shortest Seek Time First) is always the best algorithm—it can cause starvation
Confusing the direction and behavior of SCAN (elevator) and C-SCAN (circular elevator) algorithms
Thinking higher RAID levels are always better, ignoring the specific trade-offs between performance, cost, and redundancy
